title: Nothotsuga longibracteata
taxonomy: Nothotsuga longibracteata is classified as a species of coniferous tree in the cypress family (Cupressaceae). This species is native to East Asia and is a part of the genus Nothotsuga. Nothotsuga longibracteata is a relatively rare and less-studied species, but it is known to be an evergreen tree with scale-like foliage. The classification of Nothotsuga longibracteata is further supported by its morphological and molecular characteristics. It is a distinct species from other conifers in the Cupressaceae family.

intro: Nothotsuga longibracteata, also known as Korean red pine or Pinus densiflora var. longibracteata, is a coniferous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its distinctive red bark and needle-like leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions like arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Nothotsuga longibracteata include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional East Asian medicine, Nothotsuga longibracteata is typically prepared as a tea or extract, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and tinctures.
